title: Garbage bin at Ernakulam market overflowing for 5 days
description: The municipal garbage bin near Broadway Bus Stand in Ernakulam has been overflowing since Monday. Garbage is spilling onto the footpath and road. The smell is unbearable and stray dogs are scattering waste. Pedestrians are forced to walk on the road, creating a safety hazard. No collection vehicle has visited in 5 days.
rejection_reason: Complaint jurisdiction falls under private market authority, not municipal corporation. Please contact Broadway Market Management directly.

title: GHMC garbage point at Kukatpally not cleared for a week
description: The designated GHMC garbage collection point near Kukatpally Housing Board Colony Phase 3 has not been cleared in over 7 days. The pile has grown to nearly 5 feet and is blocking the entire footpath. Mosquito breeding is visible. Residents with children and elderly people are suffering. GHMC complaint number filed: GHMC-2024-KUK-0392.
landmark: KPHB Colony Phase 3 main gate, Kukatpally

title: Garbage dumped on Laxmi Nagar roadside daily by vendors
description: Vegetable and fruit vendors at Laxmi Nagar market are dumping organic waste on the roadside near Vikas Marg every night. The MCD container placed here is never emptied on time. By morning the pile is massive and attracts stray animals. Flies and mosquitoes are breeding. Nearby school children have to walk past this every day.
landmark: Vikas Marg near Laxmi Nagar Metro Station

title: Garbage bin near Golden Temple Road overflowing affecting tourism area
description: The municipal garbage bin near Hall Bazar, Amritsar — just 900 metres from Golden Temple — has been overflowing for 4 days. Tourist footfall in the area is very high and this creates a terrible impression. The garbage includes food waste and packaging that is attracting crows and stray dogs. This reflects very poorly on the city.
landmark: Hall Bazar near Golden Temple Road

title: Construction debris dumped illegally mixing with municipal waste in Wakad
description: Builders from a nearby construction site are dumping debris at the municipal garbage point on Datta Mandir Road, Wakad. This has doubled the volume of waste and the regular garbage collection truck cannot lift it. The site has been untouched for 6 days. Broken bricks, iron rods, and plaster chunks are mixed with household waste creating a hazard.
landmark: Datta Mandir Road near Wakad Signal
rejection_reason: Complaint transferred to PMC Building Permissions department. Construction debris is regulated separately under By-law No. 14C. Action will be taken against the builder directly. No action applicable on garbage department.

title: Garbage accumulation on Velachery main road near bus terminus
description: The stretch near Velachery bus terminus has turned into an open garbage dump. The two municipal bins placed there are routinely overflowing. Vendors add to the pile after market hours. Despite being a major transit hub, the area looks disgraceful. GCC trucks seem to skip this point regularly. Complaint filed on GCC app — no response for 8 days.
landmark: Near Velachery Bus Terminus, Velachery Main Road

title: Gomtinagar garbage dumping point not cleared for 9 days
description: The municipal garbage collection point near Viram Khand 3 in Gomtinagar has been neglected for 9 days. The bin is overflowing and waste has spilled over a 20-metre radius. Residents of nearby apartment complexes are extremely disturbed. Wedding function guests visiting the adjacent community hall were reportedly appalled by the state of the area.

title: Jhotwara Road garbage bin near school overflowing causing hygiene crisis
description: A municipal garbage bin placed 50 metres from Rajasthan Public School, Jhotwara Road has been overflowing for 6 days. School students walk past this every day. The bin contains decomposing food waste and the stench is severe. JMC waste vehicle skips this collection point frequently. We demand either a larger bin or daily collection.
landmark: Near Rajasthan Public School, Jhotwara Road

title: Overflowing garbage at Palayam market area causing disease risk
description: The garbage collection point at Palayam junction near the vegetable market has been completely neglected. Rotten vegetables, plastic bags, and other waste is piled up on the road for 5 days. With temperatures rising, the decomposition smell is unbearable. Flies are everywhere. Street food vendors operating nearby are concerned about hygiene and customer complaints.

title: Garbage piling at Tolichowki junction affecting nearby mosque and residents
description: The GHMC garbage collection point near Tolichowki junction has not been cleared in 8 days. Decomposing waste, including leftover food from nearby dhabas, is piled at the junction. The mosque nearby is badly affected — worshippers are complaining. The situation is worsening with summer heat. GHMC app complaint submitted (App ID GHMC-2024-TOL-1192) with no resolution.

title: Maninagar garbage zone untouched for 10 days near railway station
description: The AMC designated garbage collection area near Maninagar Railway Station has reached a breaking point. 10 days without collection. The mound of waste is now approximately 6 feet high and 15 feet wide. It blocks half of the footpath. Nearby vegetable vendors are being blamed by residents, but the actual problem is absent municipal collection.

title: Secunderabad Clock Tower area garbage point not cleaned affecting heritage zone
description: The historic Secunderabad Clock Tower surroundings are being marred by an overflowing garbage point near the main crossing. This is a heritage area and tourist zone. The garbage includes hotel waste being illegally dumped at night. GHMC inspection teams are nowhere to be seen. The area's image is being destroyed. Complaint ID GHMC-SCD-2024-0874.
rejection_reason: Garbage dumping in this area is due to commercial establishments violating BMC waste rules. Action will be initiated under GHMC Solid Waste Bye-laws. This is an enforcement matter, not a collection scheduling issue. Complaint forwarded to enforcement wing.

title: MP Nagar Zone-2 garbage point overflowing blocking pedestrian path
description: The BHOPAL Smart City project area of MP Nagar Zone-2 near the Bittan Market signal has a municipal garbage point that has not been serviced in 8 days. The overflow has blocked the pedestrian crossing. The irony is that MP Nagar is marketed as Bhopal's modern commercial hub — but this garbage situation makes it look neglected.
landmark: Near Bittan Market Signal, MP Nagar Zone-2

title: Hinjewadi Phase 1 garbage point near IT park neglected for a week
description: The municipal garbage collection point on the main road of Hinjewadi Phase 1 near the Wipro campus gate has been overflowing for 7 days. Thousands of IT employees walk past this daily. The decomposing food waste smell has entered the campus lobby according to facility managers. PMC has been notified. The irony of a Smart City IT hub having uncollected waste is glaring.
landmark: Near Wipro Campus Gate, Hinjewadi Phase 1

title: Edapally junction garbage point overflowing near NH bypass ramp
description: The garbage collection bin near the Edapally NH Bypass ramp has been overflowing for 5 days. Waste is spilling onto the footpath and partially onto the service road. The junction sees very high traffic including tourists heading toward Kochi city. The Cochin Corporation has been unresponsive. Social media posts about this have gone viral locally.
landmark: Edapally Junction near NH Bypass On-ramp

title: Deccan Gymkhana garbage bin flooding footpath near FC Road Pune
description: The municipal garbage bin at the corner of FC Road and Bhandarkar Road has been overflowing for 4 days. This is a densely populated area with cafes, coaching classes and bookshops. Bin is too small for the volume of waste generated. PMC's app-based complaint system was used (Complaint ID: PMC-SWM-2024-3310) — still showing 'under process' after 4 days.
landmark: FC Road and Bhandarkar Road Corner, Deccan

title: Habibganj area garbage bin near railway station not cleared in 7 days
description: The Habibganj (Rani Kamlapati) railway station surroundings have a municipal bin near the auto stand that has been full for 7 days. Arriving passengers from the newly renovated world-class station step out to see piles of garbage. This is embarrassing nationally. BMC's Solid Waste Management team appears to have no scheduled route covering this point.
landmark: Near Auto Stand, Habibganj Railway Station, Bhopal

title: Satellite area garbage point near Jodhpur Cross Road not cleared for a week
description: The AMC garbage collection bin near Jodhpur Cross Road in Satellite, Ahmedabad has been overflowing for 7 days. The area is an upper-middle-class residential zone and residents are shocked by the neglect. Decomposing waste is attracting large numbers of crows and stray dogs. Civic complaint on AMC portal submitted but no response after 7 days.
